case.caption: The Williams Companies, Inc. v. Energy Transfer Equity, L.P. (coordinated with C.A. No. 12337-VCG)
cause_of_action: breach of covenant of commercially reasonable efforts
breach of fiduciary duty / partnership duty (Special Issuance)
declaratory judgment (right to terminate Merger Agreement)
case.summary: After signing a Sep. 28, 2015 Merger Agreement worth tens of billions, energy markets collapsed and Energy Transfer Equity (ETE) soured on the deal. ETE's tax counsel Latham & Watkins concluded in April 2016 that it could no longer deliver the §721 tax opinion that was a closing condition. Williams sued in Chancery on two coordinated tracks: a First Action challenging ETE's defensive March 2016 'Special Issuance' of Series A Convertible Preferred Units, and a Second Action challenging Latham's inability to issue the 721 Opinion. After a two-day trial June 20–21, 2016, Vice Chancellor Glasscock ruled June 24, 2016 (the eve of the Outside Date) that Latham acted in good faith, ETE used commercially reasonable efforts, and ETE was contractually entitled to terminate the Merger Agreement on the failed closing condition.

parties.name: The Williams Companies, Inc.
representation: Kenneth J. Nachbar / Zi-Xiang Shen (Morris, Nichols, Arsht & Tunnell LLP)
Sandra C. Goldstein / Antony L. Ryan / Kevin J. Orsini (Cravath, Swaine & Moore LLP)

parties.name: Energy Transfer Equity, L.P.
representation: Rolin P. Bissell / Tammy L. Mercer / James M. Yoch, Jr. (Young Conaway Stargatt & Taylor LLP)
Michael C. Holmes / John C. Wander / Michael L. Charlson / Craig E. Zieminski (Vinson & Elkins LLP)

parties.name: Energy Transfer Corp LP, ETE Corp GP, LLC & Energy Transfer Equity GP, LLC

events.label: Merger Agreement executed
events.detail: Williams and ETE executed the Agreement and Plan of Merger on September 28, 2015. The deal contemplates Williams merging into ETC (newly formed), plus a Cash Transaction leg and a Contribution Transaction leg structured to qualify for tax-free contribution under §721(a) of the Internal Revenue Code. The §721 tax opinion from Latham is a condition precedent to closing.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Sets Outside Date of June 28, 2016 and establishes §721 Opinion as a condition to closing; governs the parties' termination rights.

events.label: Energy market decline reduces deal value
events.detail: After the Merger Agreement was entered, the energy market and the Partnership's unit price declined substantially, making the cash payment and assumed debt onerous and the Proposed Transaction undesirable to ETE.

events.label: Warren pushes to renegotiate the deal
events.detail: In December 2015, Warren led discussions within ETE that highlighted problems with the Proposed Transaction and suggested ETE seek to renegotiate the terms of the Merger Agreement.

events.label: Warren prefers terminating over restructuring
events.detail: By January 2016, according to former CFO Jamie Welch's testimony, Warren would have preferred terminating the Merger Agreement to restructuring it.

events.label: ETE announces Special Issuance of Series A Preferred Units
events.detail: On March 8, 2016 the Partnership announced it had completed a private offering (the 'Special Issuance') of Series A Convertible Preferred Units to a subset of holders (including Warren) with distribution and conversion terms running through the proposed transaction's consummation or termination. Williams contends it was a defensive measure intended to discourage the merger.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Subject of the First Action; Williams alleges it breached the Merger Agreement's non-discrimination and conduct-of-business covenants.

events.label: Whitehurst contacts Fenn about Cash Transaction structure
events.detail: On March 29, 2016, ETE in-house tax lawyer Whitehurst contacted Timothy Fenn of Latham to confirm his understanding of the Cash Transaction structure. He had recently realized that the deal called for an exchange of a fixed number of ETC shares for the cash payment — potentially driving excess cash to the Contribution leg and creating §721 problems.

events.label: Whitehurst contacts Morgan Lewis (Bill McKee)
events.detail: On April 7, 2016, Whitehurst contacted Bill McKee of Morgan Lewis to discuss the §721 issue — part of ETE's efforts to understand whether the Contribution Transaction would still qualify for tax-free treatment given the unit-price decline.

events.label: Williams files First Action (Special Issuance)
events.detail: Williams filed its first Verified Complaint in the Delaware Court of Chancery on April 6, 2016 against ETE and LE GP, challenging the Special Issuance as a breach of the Merger Agreement.

events.label: Latham conclusively determines it cannot issue 721 Opinion
events.detail: On April 11, 2016, Latham informed the Partnership that it had conclusively determined it could not provide the 721 Opinion if required to opine as of the trial-date understanding of the transaction. This determination triggered the closing-condition issue at the heart of the Second Action.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Failure of §721 Opinion is a closing-condition failure giving ETE a contractual right to terminate the Merger Agreement under §7.01(b)(1).

events.label: Court grants Motion to Expedite (First Action)
events.detail: The Court of Chancery granted Williams' motion for expedited proceedings in the First Action on April 14, 2016.

events.label: ETE announces no cash distributions through 1Q 2018
events.detail: On April 18, 2016, ETE announced it 'does not expect to make any cash distributions' through the fiscal quarter ending March 31, 2018; available cash will instead be used to make Series A in-kind distributions to Special Issuance holders.

events.label: Williams files Verified Amended Complaint (First Action)
events.detail: Williams filed its Verified Amended Complaint in the First Action on April 19, 2016.

events.label: Defendants file Answer + Counterclaim (First Action)
events.detail: On May 3, 2016, ETE and LE GP filed their Answer, Affirmative Defenses, and Verified Counterclaim in the First Action.

events.label: Williams files Second Action (721 Opinion)
events.detail: Williams filed a second Verified Complaint on May 13, 2016 against ETE, LE GP, and additional defendants ETC, ETE Corp, and ETE GP, challenging ETE's conduct concerning Latham's inability to issue the 721 Opinion.

events.label: Court grants Expedite + coordinates briefing (Second Action)
events.detail: On May 20, 2016 the Court granted Williams' Motion to Expedite the Second Action and ordered the parties to coordinate briefing of the First and Second Actions for concurrent litigation.

events.label: Defendants file Amended Defenses + Counterclaims
events.detail: On May 24, 2016, Defendants filed their Amended Affirmative Defenses and Verified Counterclaims addressing their position in both the First and Second Actions, including a request for a declaration of the right to terminate.

events.label: Two-day trial in Georgetown
events.detail: Trial of both coordinated actions was held in Georgetown, Delaware on June 20–21, 2016. The Court heard testimony from Fenn, Welch, Whitehurst, and others on Latham's good-faith determination and ETE's commercially reasonable efforts.

events.label: Supplemental briefs completed (Date Submitted)
events.detail: At the conclusion of trial, the Vice Chancellor requested supplemental briefs on certain issues; briefing was completed on June 23, 2016 — the opinion's 'Date Submitted'.

events.label: Memorandum Opinion: ETE may terminate
events.detail: Decided June 24, 2016. Glasscock, V.C. holds: (1) Latham acted in good faith in concluding it could not issue the 721 Opinion; (2) ETE did not breach the Merger Agreement's commercially-reasonable-efforts covenant; (3) accordingly ETE is contractually entitled to terminate the Merger Agreement under §7.01(b)(1) when the §721 closing condition fails, assuming Latham's opinion does not change before the June 28, 2016 Outside Date. Estoppel arguments rejected; Williams loses on both the First Action (Special Issuance) and the Second Action (721 Opinion).
events.jurisdictional_significance: Frees ETE to terminate the merger on the Outside Date and is a leading Delaware decision on conditions precedent, tax-opinion covenants, and commercially-reasonable-efforts standards.

events.label: Outside Date (merger expires)
events.detail: June 28, 2016 — the contractual Outside Date under the Merger Agreement. After this date, with the §721 closing condition unmet, ETE could terminate the Merger Agreement under §7.01(b)(1).
events.jurisdictional_significance: Contractual outside date; passage permits termination under §7.01(b)(1).

relationships.detail: The post-signing market decline is the economic backdrop that motivated ETE's reluctance to close.
Williams alleges the Special Issuance was a defensive step by Warren to discourage closing.
Williams sued in response to the Special Issuance.
Amended Complaint supersedes the original Verified Complaint.
Whitehurst's March 29 inquiry triggered the Latham re-analysis that culminated in the April 11 inability determination.
The Second Action directly challenges ETE's actions concerning Latham's §721 Opinion determination.
The opinion held the §721 closing condition would fail and ETE could terminate when the Outside Date passed.
The Merger Agreement sets the June 28, 2016 Outside Date as the contractual termination trigger under §7.01(b)(1).

sources.citation: Williams Cos. v. Energy Transfer Equity, L.P., C.A. Nos. 12168-VCG & 12337-VCG (Del. Ch. June 24, 2016) (Glasscock, V.C.) (Memorandum Opinion)
Verified Complaint, Williams Cos. v. Energy Transfer Equity, L.P., C.A. No. 12168-VCG (Del. Ch. Apr. 6, 2016)
Verified Complaint, Williams Cos. v. Energy Transfer Equity, L.P., C.A. No. 12337-VCG (Del. Ch. May 13, 2016)
